title: rubus aculeatus
intro: Rubus aculeatus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a type of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Rubus aculeatus include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and aid in the treatment of digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, can be made by infusing the leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
